Microbiome Data Visualization Teresia Mrema Buza Overview This guide is the free core for microbiome data visualization. It is designed to help you generate common plots and read them correctly. It builds on outputs from iMAP PART 08 (Exploratory Analysis) and connects visualization choices to the questions they answer. Who this is for Researchers working with 16S microbiome data outputs from QIIME2, mothur, or similar pipelines Learners who can run code but want a clearer interpretation path for plots Anyone preparing figures for manuscripts or reports What you will learn How to choose plots that match a biological or analytical question How to read composition and ordination plots without over-claiming How prevalence and filtering change what you see How to structure a reproducible visualization workflow in R Related projects iMAP project overview: https://github.com/tmbuza/imap-project-overview iMAP PART 08 (Exploratory analysis): https://github.com/tmbuza/imap-data-exploration
